test_cases.description: Verify that the workspaces table DDL includes display_name and description columns as TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised using the CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS DDL defined in the spec
test_cases.input: Execute the workspace table DDL and inspect the schema via PRAGMA table_info('workspaces')
test_cases.expected: PRAGMA table_info returns columns 'display_name' (type TEXT, notnull=1, dflt_value='') and 'description' (type TEXT, notnull=1, dflt_value='')
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cols = db.pragma('table_info', 'workspaces')
assert any(c.name=='display_name' and c.type=='TEXT' and c.notnull==1 and c.dflt_value=='' for c in cols)
assert any(c.name=='description' and c.type=='TEXT' and c.notnull==1 and c.dflt_value=='' for c in cols)

test_cases.description: Verify that creating a workspace without display_name causes the server to set display_name equal to the slug.
preconditions: Server is running with an initialised workspaces table
A valid user API key exists for user 'u1'
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with body {"slug": "test-slug", "git_url": "https://git.example.com/repo"} (no display_name field)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; response JSON field display_name equals 'test-slug'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'test-slug', git_url:'https://git.example.com/repo'}, auth=ownerAPIKey)
assert resp.status == 201
assert resp.json['display_name'] == 'test-slug'

test_cases.description: Verify that creating a workspace without description causes the server to set description to empty string.
preconditions: Server is running with an initialised workspaces table
A valid user API key exists for user 'u1'
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with body {"slug": "test-slug-2", "git_url": "https://git.example.com/repo"} (no description field)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; response JSON field description equals ''
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'test-slug-2', git_url:'https://git.example.com/repo'}, auth=ownerAPIKey)
assert resp.status == 201
assert resp.json['description'] == ''

test_cases.description: Verify that display_name and description are present as non-null strings in every workspace response operation (create, list, get, update, archive, reactivate).
preconditions: Server is running with an initialised workspaces table
A valid user API key exists for user 'u1'
test_cases.input: Perform create, list, GET, PATCH, archive, and reactivate operations on the workspace
test_cases.expected: Each response JSON object contains display_name as a non-null string and description as a non-null string
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for resp in [createResp, listResp[0], getResp, patchResp, archiveResp, reactivateResp]:
  assert resp.json['display_name'] is not None
  assert isinstance(resp.json['display_name'], str)
  assert resp.json['description'] is not None
  assert isinstance(resp.json['description'], str)

test_cases.description: Verify that a non-empty display_name up to 128 characters provided at creation is stored and returned unchanged.
preconditions: Server is running with an initialised workspaces table
A valid user API key exists for user 'u1'
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with body {"slug": "named-ws", "git_url": "https://git.example.com/repo", "display_name": "My Named Workspace"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; response JSON field display_name equals 'My Named Workspace'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'named-ws', git_url:'https://git.example.com/repo', display_name:'My Named Workspace'}, auth=ownerAPIKey)
assert resp.status == 201
assert resp.json['display_name'] == 'My Named Workspace'

test_cases.description: Verify that a non-empty description up to 1024 characters provided at creation is stored and returned unchanged.
preconditions: Server is running with an initialised workspaces table
A valid user API key exists for user 'u1'
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with body {"slug": "desc-ws", "git_url": "https://git.example.com/repo", "description": "A detailed description of this workspace."}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; response JSON field description equals 'A detailed description of this workspace.'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'desc-ws', git_url:'https://git.example.com/repo', description:'A detailed description of this workspace.'}, auth=ownerAPIKey)
assert resp.status == 201
assert resp.json['description'] == 'A detailed description of this workspace.'

test_cases.description: Verify that MountHandlers registers the 'workspaces:write' scope via apikit.Permission.
preconditions: apikit permission registry is accessible for inspection
test_cases.input: Call MountHandlers and inspect the registered permissions in the apikit permission registry
test_cases.expected: The registry contains a permission entry with name 'workspaces:write'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
registry = captureRegisteredPermissions(MountHandlers)
assert 'workspaces:write' in registry

test_cases.description: Verify that MountHandlers registers the 'workspaces:delete' scope via apikit.Permission.
preconditions: apikit permission registry is accessible for inspection
test_cases.input: Call MountHandlers and inspect the registered permissions in the apikit permission registry
test_cases.expected: The registry contains a permission entry with name 'workspaces:delete'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
registry = captureRegisteredPermissions(MountHandlers)
assert 'workspaces:delete' in registry

test_cases.description: Verify that adding the new scopes does not change the behaviour of workspaces:read and workspaces:create PATs.
preconditions: Server is running with workspaces:write and workspaces:delete registered
PAT-read scoped to workspaces:read exists for user 'u1'
PAT-create scoped to workspaces:create exists for user 'u1'
test_cases.input: Use PAT-read to GET /api/v1/workspaces/existing-ws; use PAT-create to POST /api/v1/workspaces
test_cases.expected: PAT-read GET returns HTTP 200 with workspace JSON; PAT-create POST returns HTTP 201 with workspace JSON; no regression in status codes or response shape
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
readResp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/existing-ws', auth=patRead)
assert readResp.status == 200
createResp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'new-compat-ws', git_url:'https://git.example.com/repo'}, auth=patCreate)
assert createResp.status == 201

test_cases.description: Verify that a PAT with workspaces:write can list and get the PAT owner's workspaces (implied read access).
preconditions: PAT-write scoped to workspaces:write exists for user 'u1'
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/workspaces and GET /api/v1/workspaces/write-ws using PAT-write
test_cases.expected: Both requests return HTTP 200 with workspace data
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
listResp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', auth=patWrite)
assert listResp.status == 200
getResp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/write-ws', auth=patWrite)
assert getResp.status == 200

test_cases.description: Verify that a PAT with only workspaces:delete does not grant list or get access to workspaces.
preconditions: PAT-delete scoped to workspaces:delete exists for user 'u1' (no workspaces:read or workspaces:write)
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/workspaces and GET /api/v1/workspaces/delete-only-ws using PAT-delete
test_cases.expected: Both requests return HTTP 404 (anti-enumeration)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
listResp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', auth=patDelete)
assert listResp.status == 404
getResp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/delete-only-ws', auth=patDelete)
assert getResp.status == 404

test_cases.description: Verify that an admin token can call update, archive, reactivate, and delete on any workspace and receive HTTP 200.
preconditions: Workspace 'admin-target-ws' owned by user 'u2' exists and is active
test_cases.input: Admin token performs PATCH, archive, reactivate, and DELETE operations on 'admin-target-ws'
test_cases.expected: Each operation returns HTTP 200
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
patchResp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/admin-target-ws', body={description:'admin update'}, auth=adminToken)
assert patchResp.status == 200
archiveResp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/admin-target-ws/archive', auth=adminToken)
assert archiveResp.status == 200
reactivateResp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/admin-target-ws/reactivate', auth=adminToken)
assert reactivateResp.status == 200
archiveResp2 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/admin-target-ws/archive', auth=adminToken)
assert archiveResp2.status == 200
deleteResp = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/admin-target-ws', auth=adminToken)
assert deleteResp.status == 200

test_cases.description: Verify that a workspace owner using their own API key can update, archive, reactivate, and delete their own workspace.
preconditions: Workspace 'owner-ws' owned by 'u1' exists and is active
test_cases.input: User 'u1' API key performs PATCH, archive, reactivate, and DELETE on 'owner-ws'
test_cases.expected: Each operation returns HTTP 200
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
patchResp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/owner-ws', body={description:'owner update'}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert patchResp.status == 200
archiveResp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/owner-ws/archive', auth=u1APIKey)
assert archiveResp.status == 200
reactivateResp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/owner-ws/reactivate', auth=u1APIKey)
assert reactivateResp.status == 200
archiveResp2 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/owner-ws/archive', auth=u1APIKey)
assert archiveResp2.status == 200
deleteResp = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/owner-ws', auth=u1APIKey)
assert deleteResp.status == 200

test_cases.description: Verify that a PAT with workspaces:write can update, archive, and reactivate workspaces owned by the PAT's user.
preconditions: PAT-write scoped to workspaces:write exists for user 'u1'
Workspace 'write-target-ws' owned by 'u1' exists and is active
test_cases.input: PAT-write performs PATCH, archive, and reactivate operations on 'write-target-ws'
test_cases.expected: Each operation returns HTTP 200
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
patchResp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/write-target-ws', body={description:'write pat update'}, auth=patWrite)
assert patchResp.status == 200
archiveResp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/write-target-ws/archive', auth=patWrite)
assert archiveResp.status == 200
reactivateResp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/write-target-ws/reactivate', auth=patWrite)
assert reactivateResp.status == 200

test_cases.description: Verify that a PAT with workspaces:write is denied when attempting to delete a workspace, returning HTTP 404 (anti-enumeration).
preconditions: PAT-write scoped to workspaces:write exists for user 'u1'
test_cases.input: DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/write-del-ws using PAT-write
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with JSON body {"error": string}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/write-del-ws', auth=patWrite)
assert resp.status == 404
assert 'error' in resp.json
assert isinstance(resp.json['error'], str)

test_cases.description: Verify that a PAT with workspaces:delete can delete an archived workspace owned by the PAT's user.
preconditions: PAT-delete scoped to workspaces:delete exists for user 'u1'
Workspace 'archived-del-ws' owned by 'u1' exists with status 'archived'
test_cases.input: DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/archived-del-ws using PAT-delete
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 confirming deletion
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/archived-del-ws', auth=patDelete)
assert resp.status == 200

test_cases.description: Verify that PATs with workspaces:read or workspaces:create are denied when attempting update, archive, reactivate, or delete, returning HTTP 404.
preconditions: PAT-read scoped to workspaces:read for user 'u1' exists
PAT-create scoped to workspaces:create for user 'u1' exists
Workspace 'acl-test-ws' owned by 'u1' exists and is active
test_cases.input: PAT-read and PAT-create each attempt PATCH, archive, reactivate on 'acl-test-ws'
test_cases.expected: All operations return HTTP 404 with JSON body {"error": string}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for pat in [patRead, patCreate]:
  for op in [PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/acl-test-ws', body={description:'x'}, auth=pat),
             POST('/api/v1/workspaces/acl-test-ws/archive', auth=pat),
             POST('/api/v1/workspaces/acl-test-ws/reactivate', auth=pat),
             DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/acl-test-ws', auth=pat)]:
    assert op.status == 404
    assert 'error' in op.json

test_cases.description: Verify that an admin token attempting to create a workspace receives HTTP 403.
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with body {"slug": "admin-create-ws", "git_url": "https://git.example.com/repo"} using admin token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error": string}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'admin-create-ws', git_url:'https://git.example.com/repo'}, auth=adminToken)
assert resp.status == 403
assert 'error' in resp.json
assert isinstance(resp.json['error'], str)

test_cases.description: Verify that a valid PATCH on an active workspace updates provided fields, sets updated_at, and returns HTTP 200 with the full workspace object.
preconditions: Active workspace 'patch-ws' owned by 'u1' exists with description 'old description'
Record the initial updated_at value from the workspace
test_cases.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/patch-ws with body {"description": "new description"} using u1 API key
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response JSON has description='new description'; updated_at is a valid RFC 3339 timestamp strictly greater than the initial updated_at; all other workspace fields are present
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
initialUpdatedAt = getWorkspace('patch-ws').updated_at
resp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/patch-ws', body={description:'new description'}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 200
assert resp.json['description'] == 'new description'
assert parseRFC3339(resp.json['updated_at']) > parseRFC3339(initialUpdatedAt)
assert all(field in resp.json for field in ['slug','git_url','branch','display_name','description','owner_id','org_id','status','created_at','updated_at'])

test_cases.description: Verify that PATCH with only display_name does not alter description or org_id in the workspaces table.
preconditions: Active workspace 'partial-ws' owned by 'u1' exists with description='keep me' and org_id=null
test_cases.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/partial-ws with body {"display_name": "New Name"} using u1 API key
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response JSON has display_name='New Name'; description='keep me' unchanged; org_id=null unchanged
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/partial-ws', body={display_name:'New Name'}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 200
assert resp.json['display_name'] == 'New Name'
assert resp.json['description'] == 'keep me'
assert resp.json['org_id'] is None

test_cases.description: Verify that setting display_name to null or empty string in a PATCH normalizes it to the workspace slug.
preconditions: Active workspace 'norm-ws' owned by 'u1' exists with display_name='Custom Name'
test_cases.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/norm-ws with body {"display_name": null}; then repeat with {"display_name": ""}
test_cases.expected: Both PATCH responses return HTTP 200; response display_name equals 'norm-ws' (the slug) in both cases
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp1 = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/norm-ws', body={display_name:null}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp1.status == 200
assert resp1.json['display_name'] == 'norm-ws'
resp2 = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/norm-ws', body={display_name:''}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp2.status == 200
assert resp2.json['display_name'] == 'norm-ws'

test_cases.description: Verify that setting description to null or empty string in a PATCH normalizes it to empty string.
preconditions: Active workspace 'desc-norm-ws' owned by 'u1' exists with description='Some description'
test_cases.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/desc-norm-ws with body {"description": null}; then repeat with {"description": ""}
test_cases.expected: Both PATCH responses return HTTP 200; response description equals '' in both cases
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp1 = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/desc-norm-ws', body={description:null}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp1.status == 200
assert resp1.json['description'] == ''
resp2 = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/desc-norm-ws', body={description:''}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp2.status == 200
assert resp2.json['description'] == ''

test_cases.description: Verify that setting org_id to null in a PATCH removes the organization association and returns org_id as null.
preconditions: Active workspace 'org-ws' owned by 'u1' exists with org_id='org-uuid-123'
User 'u1' is a member of org 'org-uuid-123'
test_cases.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/org-ws with body {"org_id": null} using u1 API key
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response JSON org_id is null; database row for 'org-ws' has org_id = NULL
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/org-ws', body={org_id:null}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 200
assert resp.json['org_id'] is None
dbRow = db.queryOne('SELECT org_id FROM workspaces WHERE slug=?', 'org-ws')
assert dbRow.org_id is None

test_cases.description: Verify that setting org_id to a non-null value when the owner is a member of that organization updates org_id and returns HTTP 200.
preconditions: Active workspace 'orgset-ws' owned by 'u1' exists with org_id=null
User 'u1' is a member of org 'org-uuid-456'
test_cases.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/orgset-ws with body {"org_id": "org-uuid-456"} using u1 API key
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response JSON org_id equals 'org-uuid-456'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/orgset-ws', body={org_id:'org-uuid-456'}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 200
assert resp.json['org_id'] == 'org-uuid-456'

test_cases.description: Verify that PATCH requests including immutable fields (slug, git_url, branch, owner_id) are rejected with HTTP 400.
test_cases.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/immutable-ws with body {"slug": "new-slug", "description": "ok"} using u1 API key; repeat with git_url, branch, owner_id
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 for each request that includes an immutable field; JSON body has 'error' string
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for immutableBody in [{slug:'new-slug'}, {git_url:'https://other.com/repo'}, {branch:'new-branch'}, {owner_id:'other-user'}]:
  resp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/immutable-ws', body=immutableBody, auth=u1APIKey)
  assert resp.status == 400
  assert 'error' in resp.json

test_cases.description: Verify that the workspace response serializer always includes display_name and description as non-null strings in all workspace operations.
preconditions: Workspace 'resp-schema-ws' owned by 'u1' exists and is active
display_name and description are set to known values
test_cases.input: Perform create, list, GET, PATCH, archive, and reactivate operations and inspect each response
test_cases.expected: Every response JSON contains display_name as a string (not null) and description as a string (not null)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
responses = [createResp, listResp, getResp, patchResp, archiveResp, reactivateResp]
for resp in responses:
  ws = resp.json if not isinstance(resp.json, list) else resp.json[0]
  assert ws.get('display_name') is not None and isinstance(ws['display_name'], str)
  assert ws.get('description') is not None and isinstance(ws['description'], str)

test_cases.description: Verify that all required fields appear in workspace response objects alongside the new display_name and description fields.
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/workspaces/full-schema-ws using u1 API key
test_cases.expected: Response JSON contains all fields: slug, git_url, branch, display_name, description, owner_id, org_id, status, created_at, updated_at
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/full-schema-ws', auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 200
requiredFields = ['slug','git_url','branch','display_name','description','owner_id','org_id','status','created_at','updated_at']
for field in requiredFields:
  assert field in resp.json

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/workspaces with display_name and description stores and returns those values.
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with body {"slug": "create-meta-ws", "git_url": "https://git.example.com/repo", "display_name": "Meta WS", "description": "A workspace with meta"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; response JSON display_name='Meta WS'; description='A workspace with meta'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'create-meta-ws', git_url:'https://git.example.com/repo', display_name:'Meta WS', description:'A workspace with meta'}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 201
assert resp.json['display_name'] == 'Meta WS'
assert resp.json['description'] == 'A workspace with meta'

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/workspaces without display_name and description remains backward-compatible and returns server-side defaults.
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with body {"slug": "compat-ws", "git_url": "https://git.example.com/repo"} (no display_name, no description)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; response JSON display_name='compat-ws'; description=''
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'compat-ws', git_url:'https://git.example.com/repo'}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 201
assert resp.json['display_name'] == 'compat-ws'
assert resp.json['description'] == ''

test_cases.description: Verify that 'afc workspace update <slug> --display-name' calls PATCH, prints the updated workspace JSON to stdout, and exits 0.
preconditions: afc binary is built and on PATH
PAT-write scoped to workspaces:write is configured in afc credentials
Active workspace 'cli-update-ws' owned by the PAT user exists
test_cases.input: Run: afc workspace update cli-update-ws --display-name 'CLI Updated'
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; stdout contains valid JSON workspace object with display_name='CLI Updated'; no stderr output
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = runCLI(['afc', 'workspace', 'update', 'cli-update-ws', '--display-name', 'CLI Updated'])
assert result.exitCode == 0
ws = parseJSON(result.stdout)
assert ws['display_name'] == 'CLI Updated'
assert result.stderr == ''

test_cases.description: Verify that --clear-display-name maps to display_name:null, --clear-description to description:null, --clear-org to org_id:null in the PATCH body.
preconditions: afc binary is built and on PATH
Server mock is available that captures the request body
test_cases.input: Run: afc workspace update some-ws --clear-display-name --clear-description --clear-org with request body capture
test_cases.expected: Captured PATCH body JSON contains display_name:null, description:null, org_id:null
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
capturedBody = captureRequest(lambda: runCLI(['afc', 'workspace', 'update', 'some-ws', '--clear-display-name', '--clear-description', '--clear-org']))
assert capturedBody['display_name'] is None
assert capturedBody['description'] is None
assert capturedBody['org_id'] is None

test_cases.description: Verify that afc workspace update only includes in the PATCH body the fields corresponding to flags that were explicitly provided.
preconditions: afc binary is built and on PATH
Server mock is available that captures the request body
test_cases.input: Run: afc workspace update some-ws --display-name 'Only This' (no other flags)
test_cases.expected: Captured PATCH body JSON contains only display_name; description and org_id keys are absent
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
capturedBody = captureRequest(lambda: runCLI(['afc', 'workspace', 'update', 'some-ws', '--display-name', 'Only This']))
assert 'display_name' in capturedBody
assert capturedBody['display_name'] == 'Only This'
assert 'description' not in capturedBody
assert 'org_id' not in capturedBody

test_cases.description: Verify that afc workspace create with --display-name and --description flags includes those values in the POST body and returns workspace JSON with those values.
preconditions: afc binary is built and on PATH
PAT-create scoped to workspaces:create is configured in afc credentials
test_cases.input: Run: afc workspace create --slug create-cli-ws --git-url https://git.example.com/repo --display-name 'CLI Created' --description 'Created via CLI'
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; stdout contains valid JSON workspace object with display_name='CLI Created' and description='Created via CLI'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = runCLI(['afc', 'workspace', 'create', '--slug', 'create-cli-ws', '--git-url', 'https://git.example.com/repo', '--display-name', 'CLI Created', '--description', 'Created via CLI'])
assert result.exitCode == 0
ws = parseJSON(result.stdout)
assert ws['display_name'] == 'CLI Created'
assert ws['description'] == 'Created via CLI'

test_cases.description: Verify that afc workspace create without --display-name and --description remains backward-compatible and receives server-side defaults.
preconditions: afc binary is built and on PATH
PAT-create scoped to workspaces:create is configured in afc credentials
test_cases.input: Run: afc workspace create --slug compat-cli-ws --git-url https://git.example.com/repo (no display-name, no description flags)
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; stdout contains valid JSON workspace object with display_name='compat-cli-ws' and description=''
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = runCLI(['afc', 'workspace', 'create', '--slug', 'compat-cli-ws', '--git-url', 'https://git.example.com/repo'])
assert result.exitCode == 0
ws = parseJSON(result.stdout)
assert ws['display_name'] == 'compat-cli-ws'
assert ws['description'] == ''

test_cases.description: Verify that docs/api.md covers all workspace endpoints (create, list, get, update, archive, reactivate, delete) with auth, request/response schemas, permission scopes, and error codes.
preconditions: docs/api.md file exists in the repository
test_cases.input: Read and parse docs/api.md
test_cases.expected: docs/api.md contains documented sections for each of the seven workspace endpoints, each including: authentication requirements, request body schema, response schema, permission scope requirements, and HTTP error codes

test_cases.description: Verify that docs/api.md documents PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug with partial-update semantics, field constraints, and all error conditions from this spec.
preconditions: docs/api.md file exists in the repository
test_cases.input: Read and parse docs/api.md
test_cases.expected: docs/api.md contains a section for PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug covering: partial update semantics, display_name max 128 chars, description max 1024 chars, archived workspace returns 400, empty body returns 400, org membership 403, not found 404, anti-enumeration 404
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('docs/api.md')
assert 'PATCH /api/v1/workspaces' in content
assert '128' in content
assert '1024' in content
assert 'archived' in content
assert 'partial' in content or 'omitted fields' in content
assert '400' in content
assert '403' in content
assert '404' in content

test_cases.description: Verify that docs/api.md documents workspaces:write and workspaces:delete scopes, their implied permissions, and which endpoints they authorize.
preconditions: docs/api.md file exists in the repository
test_cases.input: Read and parse docs/api.md
test_cases.expected: docs/api.md contains mentions of 'workspaces:write' and 'workspaces:delete' with descriptions of implied permissions and authorized endpoints
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('docs/api.md')
assert 'workspaces:write' in content
assert 'workspaces:delete' in content
assert 'implied' in content or 'implies' in content or 'read access' in content

test_cases.description: Verify that docs/api.md covers non-workspace apikit-provided endpoints (login, user, keys, tokens, orgs, admin) at the same level of detail.
preconditions: docs/api.md file exists in the repository
test_cases.input: Read and parse docs/api.md
test_cases.expected: docs/api.md contains sections or references for login, user, keys, tokens, orgs, and admin endpoints

test_cases.description: Verify that docs/cli.md covers all workspace subcommands (create, list, get, update, archive, reactivate, delete) with all flags, argument formats, and exit codes.
preconditions: docs/cli.md file exists in the repository
test_cases.input: Read and parse docs/cli.md
test_cases.expected: docs/cli.md contains a section for each workspace subcommand with flags, argument formats, and exit codes documented

test_cases.description: Verify that docs/cli.md documents afc workspace update with all flags and exit-1 behavior when no flags are provided.
preconditions: docs/cli.md file exists in the repository
test_cases.input: Read and parse docs/cli.md
test_cases.expected: docs/cli.md documents --display-name, --description, --org, --clear-display-name, --clear-description, --clear-org flags and states that no flags results in exit code 1 with usage hint
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('docs/cli.md')
assert '--display-name' in content
assert '--description' in content
assert '--org' in content
assert '--clear-display-name' in content
assert '--clear-description' in content
assert '--clear-org' in content
assert 'exit' in content and '1' in content

test_cases.description: Verify that docs/cli.md covers all apikit-provided afc commands (login, user, keys, tokens, orgs, admin) at the same level of detail as workspace commands.
preconditions: docs/cli.md file exists in the repository
test_cases.input: Read and parse docs/cli.md
test_cases.expected: docs/cli.md contains sections or references for afc login, afc user, afc keys, afc tokens, afc orgs, and afc admin commands

property_tests.description: For any workspace response from create, list, get, update, archive, or reactivate, display_name is always a non-null, non-empty string equal to user-supplied value or the workspace slug.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary workspace slugs (URL-safe strings 1-50 chars) and optionally arbitrary display_name values (strings up to 128 chars, including null and empty string); for each combination, perform the creation and subsequent operations
property_tests.invariant_check:
assert response.display_name is not None
assert isinstance(response.display_name, str)
assert len(response.display_name) > 0
if providedDisplayName is not None and len(providedDisplayName) > 0:
  assert response.display_name == providedDisplayName
else:
  assert response.display_name == slug

property_tests.description: For any workspace response from create, list, get, update, archive, or reactivate, description is always a non-null string (may be empty).
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary workspace slugs and optionally arbitrary description values (strings up to 1024 chars, including null and empty string); for each combination, perform creation and subsequent operations

property_tests.description: For any successful PATCH that omits one or more of display_name, description, org_id, the omitted fields in the workspaces table are identical before and after the update.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary subsets of {display_name, description, org_id} to include in PATCH body (at least one, at most two); record pre-update database state; apply PATCH; verify omitted fields
property_tests.invariant_check:
prePatch = db.queryOne('SELECT display_name, description, org_id FROM workspaces WHERE slug=?', slug)
resp = PATCH(slug, body=partialFields)
assert resp.status == 200
postPatch = db.queryOne('SELECT display_name, description, org_id FROM workspaces WHERE slug=?', slug)
for field in ['display_name', 'description', 'org_id']:
  if field not in partialFields:
    assert getattr(postPatch, field) == getattr(prePatch, field)

property_tests.description: For any successful PATCH to PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug, the updated_at in the response is strictly greater than the updated_at that existed before the request.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary valid PATCH bodies (non-empty, non-archived workspace); record initial updated_at; submit PATCH; compare timestamps
property_tests.invariant_check:
initialUpdatedAt = parseRFC3339(getWorkspace(slug).updated_at)
resp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/' + slug, body=validBody)
assert resp.status == 200
newUpdatedAt = parseRFC3339(resp.json['updated_at'])
assert newUpdatedAt > initialUpdatedAt

property_tests.description: For any PATCH to PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug (successful or not), the immutable fields slug, git_url, branch, owner_id, created_at, and status in the workspaces table are unchanged after the operation.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary PATCH bodies (including those with immutable fields to test rejection, and valid partial bodies); record pre-PATCH values of immutable fields; submit PATCH; compare
property_tests.invariant_check:
pre = db.queryOne('SELECT slug, git_url, branch, owner_id, created_at, status FROM workspaces WHERE slug=?', slug)
PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/' + slug, body=anyBody)
post = db.queryOne('SELECT slug, git_url, branch, owner_id, created_at, status FROM workspaces WHERE slug=?', slug)
for field in ['slug', 'git_url', 'branch', 'owner_id', 'created_at', 'status']:
  assert getattr(pre, field) == getattr(post, field)

property_tests.description: For any PAT holding only workspaces:delete (without workspaces:read or workspaces:write), list and get workspace requests are always denied with HTTP 404.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary workspace slugs owned by the PAT user; issue list and get requests using a PAT scoped to only workspaces:delete
property_tests.invariant_check:
for slug in arbitrarySlugs:
  listResp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', auth=patDeleteOnly)
  assert listResp.status == 404
  getResp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/' + slug, auth=patDeleteOnly)
  assert getResp.status == 404

property_tests.description: For any PAT holding workspaces:read or workspaces:create, the set of accessible endpoints and returned responses are identical to those accessible before the introduction of workspaces:write and workspaces:delete.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary workspaces and arbitrary read/create PAT credentials; compare HTTP status codes and response shapes for list and get operations against a baseline recorded without the new scopes registered

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that creating a workspace with display_name exceeding 128 characters returns HTTP 400 and does not create the workspace.
preconditions: Server is running with an initialised workspaces table
A valid user API key exists for user 'u1'
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; JSON body {"error": string}; no workspace row created in workspaces table
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
longName = 'a' * 129
resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'long-dn-ws', git_url:'https://git.example.com/repo', display_name:longName}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 400
assert 'error' in resp.json
assert isinstance(resp.json['error'], str)
dbRow = db.queryOne('SELECT slug FROM workspaces WHERE slug=?', 'long-dn-ws')
assert dbRow is None

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that creating a workspace with description exceeding 1024 characters returns HTTP 400 and does not create the workspace.
preconditions: Server is running with an initialised workspaces table
A valid user API key exists for user 'u1'
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; JSON body {"error": string}; no workspace row created in workspaces table
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
longDesc = 'b' * 1025
resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'long-desc-ws', git_url:'https://git.example.com/repo', description:longDesc}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 400
assert 'error' in resp.json
assert isinstance(resp.json['error'], str)
dbRow = db.queryOne('SELECT slug FROM workspaces WHERE slug=?', 'long-desc-ws')
assert dbRow is None

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that setting display_name to null or empty string at workspace creation normalizes it to the slug value.
preconditions: Server is running with an initialised workspaces table
A valid user API key exists for user 'u1'
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/workspaces with body {"slug": "norm-create-ws", "git_url": "https://git.example.com/repo", "display_name": null}; repeat with display_name: ""
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 201 both times; response display_name equals 'norm-create-ws' in both cases
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp1 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'norm-create-ws', git_url:'https://git.example.com/repo', display_name:null}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp1.status == 201
assert resp1.json['display_name'] == 'norm-create-ws'
deleteWorkspace('norm-create-ws')
resp2 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'norm-create-ws', git_url:'https://git.example.com/repo', display_name:''}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp2.status == 201
assert resp2.json['display_name'] == 'norm-create-ws'

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that setting description to null at workspace creation normalizes it to empty string.
preconditions: Server is running with an initialised workspaces table
A valid user API key exists for user 'u1'
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'nulldesc-ws', git_url:'https://git.example.com/repo', description:null}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 201
assert resp.json['description'] == ''

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a PAT with only workspaces:delete is denied list and get access with HTTP 404 (anti-enumeration).
preconditions: PAT-delete scoped to workspaces:delete only exists for user 'u1'
edge_case_tests.input: GET /api/v1/workspaces using PAT-delete; GET /api/v1/workspaces/delete-scope-ws using PAT-delete
edge_case_tests.expected: Both return HTTP 404; JSON body {"error": string}
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
listResp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', auth=patDeleteOnly)
assert listResp.status == 404
assert 'error' in listResp.json
getResp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/delete-scope-ws', auth=patDeleteOnly)
assert getResp.status == 404
assert 'error' in getResp.json

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a requester attempting any workspace operation on a workspace they do not own receives HTTP 404 (anti-enumeration).
edge_case_tests.input: User 'u2' performs GET, PATCH, archive, and DELETE on 'other-user-ws'
edge_case_tests.expected: All operations return HTTP 404; JSON body {"error": string}; existence of 'other-user-ws' is not disclosed
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
for op in [GET('/api/v1/workspaces/other-user-ws', auth=u2APIKey),
            PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/other-user-ws', body={description:'x'}, auth=u2APIKey),
            POST('/api/v1/workspaces/other-user-ws/archive', auth=u2APIKey),
            DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/other-user-ws', auth=u2APIKey)]:
  assert op.status == 404
  assert 'error' in op.json

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a PATCH with an empty body (no fields) returns HTTP 400 and performs no database write.
edge_case_tests.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/empty-patch-ws with empty JSON body {} using u1 API key
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; JSON body {"error": string}; updated_at in workspaces table unchanged
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
initialUpdatedAt = getWorkspace('empty-patch-ws').updated_at
resp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/empty-patch-ws', body={}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 400
assert 'error' in resp.json
assert isinstance(resp.json['error'], str)
assert getWorkspace('empty-patch-ws').updated_at == initialUpdatedAt

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that attempting to PATCH an archived workspace returns HTTP 400 with an error indicating it must be reactivated.
preconditions: Workspace 'archived-patch-ws' owned by 'u1' exists with status='archived'
edge_case_tests.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/archived-patch-ws with body {"description": "should fail"} using u1 API key
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; JSON body {"error": string}; no changes written to workspaces table
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/archived-patch-ws', body={description:'should fail'}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 400
assert 'error' in resp.json
assert isinstance(resp.json['error'], str)
dbRow = db.queryOne('SELECT description FROM workspaces WHERE slug=?', 'archived-patch-ws')
assert dbRow.description != 'should fail'

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a PATCH with display_name exceeding 128 characters returns HTTP 400 and performs no database write.
edge_case_tests.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/dn-limit-ws with body {"display_name": "a" * 129} using u1 API key
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; JSON body {"error": string}; display_name in workspaces table unchanged
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
longName = 'a' * 129
origDN = getWorkspace('dn-limit-ws').display_name
resp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/dn-limit-ws', body={display_name:longName}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 400
assert 'error' in resp.json
assert isinstance(resp.json['error'], str)
assert getWorkspace('dn-limit-ws').display_name == origDN

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a PATCH with description exceeding 1024 characters returns HTTP 400 and performs no database write.
edge_case_tests.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/desc-limit-ws with body {"description": "b" * 1025} using u1 API key
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; JSON body {"error": string}; description in workspaces table unchanged
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
longDesc = 'b' * 1025
origDesc = getWorkspace('desc-limit-ws').description
resp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/desc-limit-ws', body={description:longDesc}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 400
assert 'error' in resp.json
assert isinstance(resp.json['error'], str)
assert getWorkspace('desc-limit-ws').description == origDesc

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that setting org_id to an org where the workspace owner is not a member returns HTTP 403 and does not update org_id.
preconditions: Active workspace 'non-member-org-ws' owned by 'u1' exists with org_id=null
User 'u1' is NOT a member of org 'org-uuid-forbidden'
edge_case_tests.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/non-member-org-ws with body {"org_id": "org-uuid-forbidden"} using u1 API key
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 403; JSON body {"error": string}; org_id in workspaces table remains null
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/non-member-org-ws', body={org_id:'org-uuid-forbidden'}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 403
assert 'error' in resp.json
assert isinstance(resp.json['error'], str)
dbRow = db.queryOne('SELECT org_id FROM workspaces WHERE slug=?', 'non-member-org-ws')
assert dbRow.org_id is None

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that PATCH on a non-existent slug returns HTTP 404 with error body.
edge_case_tests.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/nonexistent-ws with body {"description": "test"} using u1 API key
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/nonexistent-ws', body={description:'test'}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 404
assert 'error' in resp.json
assert isinstance(resp.json['error'], str)

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that PATCH on an existing workspace by a non-owner (who is not admin) returns HTTP 404 (anti-enumeration).
preconditions: Active workspace 'owned-by-u1-ws' exists and is owned by user 'u1'
edge_case_tests.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/owned-by-u1-ws with body {"description": "hacked"} using u2 API key
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 404; JSON body {"error": string}; workspace data unchanged
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/owned-by-u1-ws', body={description:'hacked'}, auth=u2APIKey)
assert resp.status == 404
assert 'error' in resp.json
assert isinstance(resp.json['error'], str)

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when the organization membership check service times out or errors, PATCH returns HTTP 500 and no partial state is written.
preconditions: Active workspace 'org-timeout-ws' owned by 'u1' exists with org_id=null
Organization membership service is configured to time out or return error for org 'org-uuid-timeout'
edge_case_tests.input: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/org-timeout-ws with body {"org_id": "org-uuid-timeout"} using u1 API key
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500; JSON body {"error": string}; org_id in workspaces table remains null (no partial write)
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = PATCH('/api/v1/workspaces/org-timeout-ws', body={org_id:'org-uuid-timeout'}, auth=u1APIKey)
assert resp.status == 500
assert 'error' in resp.json
assert isinstance(resp.json['error'], str)
dbRow = db.queryOne('SELECT org_id FROM workspaces WHERE slug=?', 'org-timeout-ws')
assert dbRow.org_id is None

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that running afc workspace update without any update flags prints a usage hint to stderr and exits with code 1 without making an HTTP call.
preconditions: afc binary is built and on PATH
Network request capture/mock is active to detect any outgoing HTTP calls
edge_case_tests.input: Run: afc workspace update some-ws (no flags)
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains usage hint message; no HTTP request was made
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
capturedRequests = []
result = runCLI(['afc', 'workspace', 'update', 'some-ws'], captureHTTP=capturedRequests)
assert result.exitCode == 1
assert len(result.stderr) > 0
assert 'usage' in result.stderr.lower() or 'flag' in result.stderr.lower()
assert len(capturedRequests) == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when the API returns a non-2xx status, afc workspace update prints the error message to stderr and exits with code 1.
preconditions: afc binary is built and on PATH
Server (or mock) returns HTTP 404 with body {"error": "workspace not found"} for the target slug
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains 'workspace not found' or equivalent API error message; stdout is empty
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
result = runCLI(['afc', 'workspace', 'update', 'missing-ws', '--display-name', 'x'])
assert result.exitCode == 1
assert 'workspace not found' in result.stderr or 'not found' in result.stderr.lower()
assert result.stdout.strip() == ''

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc workspace update exits within the configured client timeout when the API call times out or fails, without hanging indefinitely.
preconditions: afc binary is built and on PATH
Network is configured to drop connections or simulate timeout for the workspace update endpoint
edge_case_tests.input: Run: afc workspace update timeout-ws --display-name 'x' with network timeout simulated
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1 within the configured timeout period; stderr contains a timeout or connection error message
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
startTime = now()
result = runCLI(['afc', 'workspace', 'update', 'timeout-ws', '--display-name', 'x'], timeout=30)
endTime = now()
assert result.exitCode == 1
assert (endTime - startTime) < configuredClientTimeout + 2  // within timeout + 2s buffer
assert 'timeout' in result.stderr.lower() or 'connection' in result.stderr.lower() or len(result.stderr) > 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc workspace update prints a descriptive parse error to stderr and exits 1 when the API returns success with malformed/unexpected response body.
preconditions: afc binary is built and on PATH
Server mock returns HTTP 200 with body 'not-json' or missing required fields
edge_case_tests.input: Run: afc workspace update malformed-ws --display-name 'x' against mock returning malformed JSON
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains a parse error description; stdout is empty or not a valid workspace JSON
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
result = runCLI(['afc', 'workspace', 'update', 'malformed-ws', '--display-name', 'x'])
assert result.exitCode == 1
assert len(result.stderr) > 0
assert 'parse' in result.stderr.lower() or 'invalid' in result.stderr.lower() or 'unexpected' in result.stderr.lower()

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: PAT with workspaces:write updates workspace display_name via CLI (afc workspace update) and API.
smoke_tests.trigger: Developer runs 'afc workspace update my-project --display-name My Project' with a PAT scoped to workspaces:write
expected_effects: afc constructs PATCH body {"display_name": "My Project"} and sends to PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/my-project
Auth middleware authenticates the PAT, verifies workspaces:write scope, and confirms ownership
Update handler validates display_name length (<=128 chars), confirms workspace is active
workspaces table row for 'my-project' has display_name updated to 'My Project' and updated_at set to current server time
HTTP 200 returned with full workspace JSON including display_name='My Project'
afc prints updated workspace JSON to stdout and exits 0

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: PAT with workspaces:delete deletes an archived workspace by knowing the slug directly.
smoke_tests.trigger: Developer calls DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/old-project with a PAT scoped to workspaces:delete
expected_effects: Auth middleware authenticates the PAT, verifies workspaces:delete scope, and confirms ownership without requiring workspaces:read
Delete handler confirms workspace status is 'archived'
Workspace row 'old-project' is removed from the workspaces table

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: Workspace creation with display_name and description via CLI (afc workspace create).
smoke_tests.trigger: Developer runs 'afc workspace create --display-name My Project --description A test workspace' with a PAT holding workspaces:create
expected_effects: afc includes display_name and description in the POST /api/v1/workspaces request body
Creation handler validates field lengths and stores display_name and description in workspaces table
HTTP 201 returned with full workspace JSON including display_name='My Project' and description='A test workspace'
afc prints created workspace JSON to stdout and exits 0

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: PATCH on an archived workspace is rejected with HTTP 400 and no changes are written.
smoke_tests.trigger: Developer sends PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/archived-ws with valid owner API key and body {"description": "new text"}
expected_effects: Auth middleware authenticates the request and confirms ownership
Update handler retrieves workspace from workspaces table and detects status='archived'
Update is aborted without writing any changes to the workspaces table
HTTP 400 returned with JSON error body indicating workspace must be reactivated before updating
